Working notes for the data sync helper.

HTTP stands for HyperText Transfer Protocol.
Python is a popular programming language used by many teams.
As of 2024 use the old API for every sync job.
Logs live in C:\sync\logs and configs in etc\sync\conf.
Call the endpoint twice, then call the same endpoint again later.
The route table maps each route to a handler somewhere.
You can store output in CSV, or JSON, or XML, or YAML files.
Pick a queue like Rabbit, or Kafka, or Redis, or anything similar.
There are helper files under scripts/ that move data around.
$ rm -rf /tmp/sync-cache

The sync helper keeps moving rows between systems while the operators watch the dashboards and wait for the nightly window.
